title: ptelea trifoliata subsp. pallida
intro: Ptelea trifoliata subsp. pallida, also known as the white prickly ash, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its unique, fragrant, and bitter properties. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Ptelea trifoliata subsp. pallida has been valued for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve respiratory issues and reduce pain and swelling. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory infections, as well as its potential to support digestive health and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Ptelea trifoliata subsp. pallida can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
